metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either tag AC3 as `[operator-attested]` (per ADR 0043) and record a concrete attestation (timestamp + observed failure output) on the ticket, or add a scripted proof step (e.g. a git-stash/patch-revert script that runs pytest and asserts non-zero exit) so the check is reproducible.
evidence: 'verified locally, not committed' — explicit acknowledgement that no committed artifact captures the result
No pytest command or CI step is associated with AC3
The criterion is a one-time local revert-and-observe, not a durable repo state
findings.finding: AC3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix makes the parametrized depends_on case FAIL — verified locally, not committed') has no runnable proving command that produces durable, auditable evidence; it is an ephemeral manual step whose outcome cannot be reproduced or verified in a later session or by a reviewing agent.
scenarios: An executor skips AC3 (or performs it incorrectly) with no way for a reviewer to confirm it was done; the new parametrized test could be written in a way that passes even WITHOUT the c8ed fix present (e.g. asserting the wrong tuple), silently defeating the anti-regression purpose.
findings.suggested_fix: Tag the AC line: `[operator-attested] Reverting the c8ed fix (re-drop the swap) makes the parametrized depends_on case FAIL — record the failing pytest output as a comment on this ticket.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a described work item in Scope (or a note in AC line 3) explaining how the local revert verification is documented or demonstrated — e.g. a short comment in the test file, a CI mutation-test step, or an explicit 'author attests' note — so the criterion is not simply unverifiable.
evidence: AC line 3 says 'verified locally, not committed'
The Scope section lists two file-level deliverables (test_link_dedup.py, test_link_removal_sync.py) but mentions no revert/regression-proof step
ABSENCE: no Scope bullet describes how or when this local verification is performed or recorded
findings.finding: AC line 3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix … verified locally, not committed') describes a verification exercise with no corresponding described work in the Scope section and no CI-verifiable proof, making it an orphan criterion that an implementer cannot demonstrate in a PR or automated run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix …')
scenarios: A reviewer or CI system has no way to confirm this criterion was satisfied
An implementer may skip it entirely since it produces no committed artifact and is not checked by any named command
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add a bullet such as: 'Locally verify that temporarily dropping the `swap=True` in outbound_links.py causes the parametrized `depends_on` case to FAIL (author attests in PR description; not committed).' Then update AC line 3 to say 'Author attests in PR description that this local check passed' so reviewers know the expected evidence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the line reference for the REMOVE path's direction logic from `outbound_links.py:193-202` to `outbound_links.py:198-205` (the `if inward_key / elif outward_key / depends_on` branch).
evidence: outbound_links.py line 192-193: `if base_relation is None: continue` — this is an unmapped-type guard, not the inward/outward branch
outbound_links.py lines 198-205 contain the actual `if inward_key: ... elif outward_key: relation = 'depends_on' if base_relation == 'blocks' else base_relation` logic
findings.finding: The plan cites outbound_links.py:193-202 as the location of the REMOVE path's inward/outward direction logic, but the actual inward/outward branch (if inward_key: / elif outward_key: / relation = 'depends_on' if base_relation == 'blocks') runs from line 198 to line 205. Line 193 is a `continue` for unmapped link types, not the direction logic.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the untested branch of the REMOVE path's own inward/outward direction logic at outbound_links.py:193-202'
scenarios: An implementer or reviewer looking at lines 193-202 to understand what the new REMOVE test covers will find an off-by-5 mismatch; the actual direction-disambiguation logic starts at line 198.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the untested branch of the REMOVE path's own inward/outward direction logic at outbound_links.py:193-202' to 'outbound_links.py:198-205'.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cardinality guard to AC1's proving command (e.g. `pytest --collect-only -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py | grep 'test_outbound_emission' | wc -l` asserted equal to 6, or use `pytest -v` and grep for all 6 relation names) so the command fails if fewer than 6 parametrized cases are collected.
evidence: 'An outbound emission test is parametrized over all 6 relations: the 3 mapped ones... the 3 unmapped ones assert an empty mutation list.'
Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py -q` — no `--collect-only | grep -c` or parametrize-count guard
findings.finding: AC1's proving command (`pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py -q`) carries no cardinality assertion, so an executor who writes fewer than 6 parametrized cases (e.g. only the 3 mapped ones) would see the command pass while the criterion ('parametrized over all 6 relations') is unmet.
scenarios: An executor implements only the 3 mapped parametrized cases, omitting the 3 unmapped ones; the pytest run exits 0 and the AC is signed off, leaving the unmapped-relation gap open.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1's proof step: `pytest --collect-only -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py | grep -E '\[(blocks|depends_on|relates_to|duplicates|supersedes|discovered_from)\]' | wc -l` and assert the output is `6`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the parametrize approach to import the mapped relations directly from `outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` (e.g. `@pytest.mark.parametrize('relation,expected', list(_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK.items()) + [(r, None) for r in UNMAPPED])`) so the test list is driven by the canonical source and a new mapping entry is automatically covered.
evidence: Plan states the goal is 'so every current AND future relation is covered (no more single-representative blind spots)'
outbound_links.py comment: 'Re-declare a local copy here … Keep in sync with acli_graph' — vocabulary is duplicated with a manual-sync comment, not a single source
A hardcoded parametrize list of 6 relations in the test file cannot self-update when the mapping grows
ABSENCE: the plan specifies no mechanism that would catch vocabulary drift between the hardcoded test list and the production mapping
findings.finding: The plan claims parametrization will cover 'every current AND future relation', but the proposed parametrize list is a hardcoded 6-item literal in the test file rather than being driven by the canonical `_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` vocabulary source. If a new mappable relation is added to `outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` (or `acli_graph._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK`) in the future, the parametrized test passes green while the new relation is untested — the very gap class the plan claims to close.
findings.location: Scope section / AC1: 'parametrize outbound _diff_links emission over ALL relations' / outbound_links.py:35-39 (_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK local copy)
scenarios: A new relation (e.g. 'caused_by') is added to _RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK in outbound_links.py; the parametrized test still passes (it only asserts the hardcoded 6); the new relation is untested — re-opening the blind-spot class
findings.suggested_fix: In `test_link_dedup.py`, derive the parametrize inputs from the production dict rather than hardcoding them: load `outbound_links` (already done via the module fixture), then use `outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK.items()` for the mapped cases and compute the unmapped set as `ALL_RELATIONS - set(outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK)` (where ALL_RELATIONS is imported from `rebar.types` or the schema). This keeps the test aligned with the production vocabulary automatically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the plan for the hardcoded-list parametrize approach (e.g. 'chosen for simplicity since the relation vocabulary is small and stable; acceptable because AC3 verifies semantic coupling') so implementers and reviewers understand the design intent.
evidence: ABSENCE: the plan contains no sentence explaining why a hardcoded 6-item parametrize list was preferred over a dict-driven approach or a vocabulary-sync contract test
The 'Why' section explains the bug motivation but not the test design choice
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for WHY the parametrize-over-hardcoded-list approach was chosen — it describes WHAT to do but not WHY this design is better than (for example) a vocabulary-driven parametrize that imports the mapping dict, or a contract test asserting the two `_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK` copies stay in sync.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope sections — no rationale for approach selection present
scenarios: A reviewer or future implementer reading the plan cannot distinguish whether the hardcoded list was a deliberate simplicity trade-off or an oversight — leading to either over-engineering or under-engineering the solution

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
